description.en: The byte expected at offset in the frame (reject the frame otherwise). Named expect, not value — it's the byte the frame must carry, distinct from the many other value concepts.
Command prefix that requests a parameter's current value (M4-ATX: [0xa4, 0xa1]); index/length bytes overlaid at their offsets, rest zero-padded to request_length.
Where the addressed parameter's index byte sits in the command (M4-ATX: 2).
Where the parameter's byte-length sits in the command (M4-ATX: 3) — the device echoes the width it should read/write.
Zero-based byte offset into a request/response frame. The single place the key is defined — diag byte-fields, magic-byte checks and firmware-version bytes all compose it.
The addressable settings — array of usbhid_params items.
Fixed length of every config command/response buffer (M4-ATX: 24).
A leading sanity byte-check a device stamps on a valid frame/response, as { offset, expect }. Shared by the usbhid diag read and config exchange.
Command prefix that writes a parameter (M4-ATX: [0xa4, 0xa0]); index/length/value overlaid. The device echoes index+value for the writer to verify.
Where the VALUE bytes sit — in the GET response (decode from here) and the SET request (encode to here), big-endian per the parameter's byte_order (M4-ATX: 4).
